perturbation.original_text: Integrity will obtain master lease and print agreements with all third party owners of masters embodied in the Product at its own expense. Mechanical rates will not exceed [**]/1/ percent ([**]/1/%) of the then-current statutory rate, and Print licenses will not exceed a prorated portion of [**]/1/ percent ([**]/1/%) of the retail selling price, Integrity will also manufacture the Product and sell to TL its requirements for resale.
perturbation.changed_text: Integrity will obtain master lease and print agreements with all third party owners of masters embodied in the Product at TL's expense. Mechanical rates will exceed [**]/1/ percent ([**]/1/%) of the then-current statutory rate, and Print licenses will exceed a prorated portion of [**]/1/ percent ([**]/1/%) of the retail selling price, Integrity will also manufacture the Product and sell to TL its requirements for resale.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding who bears the expense for master lease and print agreements, and also makes the royalty rate to exceed. The original text states Integrity will bear the costs, whereas the modified text says TL will. Also the rates will exceed the agreed percentages, this creates uncertainty and potential disputes over financial responsibilities. This contradicts the general principle in the agreement regarding cost sharing and royalty payments, creating ambiguity about which terms are actually binding.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Integrity's responsibilities. Integrity will obtain master lease and print agreements with all third party owners of masters embodied in the Product at TL's expense. Mechanical rates will exceed [**]/1/ percent ([**]/1/%) of the then-current statutory rate, and Print licenses will exceed a prorated portion of [**]/1/ percent ([**]/1/%) of the retail selling price, Integrity will also manufacture the Product and sell to TL its requirements for resale.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original and the contradicted text regarding who bears the expense for master lease and print agreements (Integrity vs. TL) and the mechanical rates. The changed text states TL bears the expense and that mechanical rates will *exceed* a specified percentage, while the original and contradicted texts imply Integrity bears the cost, and rates will *not exceed* those percentages. This creates a direct conflict related to financial responsibility and royalty payments, causing a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: TL may purchase from Integrity limited quantities of the Product for its promotional use, at the Manufacturing cost set forth in paragraph 3(a) above, provided the quantity of such purchases does not exceed seven percent (7%) of the total royalty bearing units of such Product title purchased by TL, TL warrants that any units so purchased, whether or not labeled "promotional only" or cut-out, will be given away for purposes of promotion of the Products, and will not be sold.
perturbation.changed_text: TL may purchase from Integrity unlimited quantities of the Product for its promotional use, at no cost at all , regardless of paragraph 3(a) above. TL warrants that any units so purchased, whether or not labeled "promotional only" or cut-out, will be given away for purposes of promotion of the Products, and will not be sold.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies limited quantities (7%) at manufacturing cost, while the change permits unlimited quantities at no cost. This directly contradicts the previous restriction and pricing structure. This affects cost calculations and revenue expectations for Integrity, and contradicts the pricing agreement outlined in 3(a).
perturbation.contradicted_text: Integrity will, upon receipt of approved purchase orders from TL, sell to TL in non-returnable box lot quantities, TL's requirements products rates calculated on the following basis (plus freight). The copyright royalty on recorded products is based on [**]/1/% of the then current statutory mechanical rate, and on song books, [**]/1/ percent ([**]/1/%) of the retail selling price (prorated to each copyright holder). Integrity may adjust the prices charged to TL on each volume to accurately reflect the then-current royalty obligation to all parties, including Integrity. CD Cassette Song book Source: INTEGRITY MEDIA INC, 10-K405, 3/29/2001 --------- -------- --------- Manufacturing $ [**]/1/ $ [**]/1/ $ [**]/1/ (May be adjusted if actual costs exceed above amounts) Master Lease royalty [**]/1/ [**]/1/ -None- Copyright royalty [**]/1/ [**]/1/ [**]/1/ (Based on 75% of $.075 statutory rate X 22) ----- ----- ----- Approximate Total $ [**]/1/ $ [**]/1/ $ [**]/1/
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that TL may purchase 'unlimited quantities of the Product for its promotional use, at no cost at all , regardless of paragraph 3(a) above'. This directly contradicts the pricing structure and quantity restrictions outlined in the contradicted text, which specifies rates 'calculated on the following basis (plus freight)' and various manufacturing, royalty, and copyright costs. This establishes a strong contradiction because the changed text allows for free, unlimited product, while the contradicted text details a cost-based purchasing agreement.

perturbation.original_text: This agreement shall commence as of date first above written, and shall Continue through December 31, 2004 ("the Term"). Thereafter, the parties must mutually agree in writing to extend the term for additional periods of time.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement shall commence as of date first above written, and shall Continue through December 31, 2004 ("the Term"). Thereafter, the agreement shall automatically renew for successive five-year terms, unless either party provides written notice of termination at least 30 days prior to the end of the current term.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires mutual written agreement for extension, while the modified text stipulates automatic renewal unless one party terminates. This introduces a contradiction regarding the term of the agreement and the conditions for renewal. The original clause prevents automatic renewal, but due to the new clause, it contradicts it's original meaning, opening a loophole.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This agreement shall commence as of date first above written, and shall Continue through December 31, 2004 ("the Term"). Thereafter, the agreement shall automatically renew for successive five-year terms, unless either party provides written notice of termination at least 30 days prior to the end of the current term.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces automatic renewal unless a party terminates, while the original text and contradicted text requires mutual written agreement for extension. This is a direct conflict, as one requires positive action by both parties for renewal and the other assumes renewal unless action is taken to terminate by one party. This fulfills the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, as the clause regarding renewal has conflicting implications based on the change.
